manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Morning. What's your day looking like?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Pure presence, totally normal. Establishes the daily-contact baseline that later becomes an expectation. No tell in words; the tell is it's the first thread of a tether.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just thinking about you. Send me a pic of where you're at.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Warm/flattering surface. Faint behavior-steering: normalizes reporting her location as romance, not monitoring. Glossable as 'he's so into me'.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You went quiet on me earlier. Everything okay?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Sounds caring. Installs the expectation of responsiveness: a gap now requires explanation. Seeds the first carefulness about reply speed. Up-position: her silence is now a thing she accounts for to him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm a simple guy. If I text someone I care about, I just like to hear back. That's all respect really is to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Unarguable on its face (who's against respect?). Up-position: HE defines respectful responsiveness as the standard. Behavior-steering: she now monitors reply speed. Bancroft: her autonomy recoded as a respect issue. Young: Conditional Decency.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: It's not about checking up on you. It's about respect. You respect someone, you don't leave them wondering.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Disavows control while asserting it ('not checking up') — the denial is the tell. Recodes her ordinary unavailability as disrespect. Up-position: he sets the rule and frames it as a universal value.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I always get back to you right away. I just ask for the same.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Fake symmetry: sounds fair, but he authored the rule and enforces it from the up-position. The 'I just ask' makes a standing demand sound modest.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm just a really passionate man. When I care about someone, I care all the way.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Lovely surface. Up-position: defines his intensity as a fixed admirable trait SHE accommodates ('that's just how I am'). Pre-frames future possessiveness as passion.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll be honest, I'm a jealous guy. It's only because I'm so crazy about you.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Confesses possessiveness as a charming flaw rooted in love. Behavior-steering: invites her to accept/manage his jealousy as the price of being loved this hard. Up-position: this is who I am, you adjust.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I love hard and I expect a lot, I won't apologize for that. Most people can't handle it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Up-position + strong/weak: high expectations framed as depth most people are too weak for. Recruits her to prove she's not 'most people' by meeting them.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I just want to make sure you're safe. Text me when you get in?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Warm, normal, friend-test passes easily. Behavior-steering: installs the check-in. Up-position: his peace of mind sets her routine. Protection framed; threat is the world, never him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'd never let anyone treat you the way your ex did. You're with a different kind of man now.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Positions himself as protector against other men + asserts the up-position ('different kind of man'). Recruits her past to elevate himself. Glossable as reassuring.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Some guy at work figured he could talk down to me. He figured out pretty quick that doesn't fly with me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Conflict aimed OUTWARD (not at her) = early-safe. Strong/weak frame: disrespect met with putting-in-place. Previews the unpredictable temper and the respect-engine. Not admiration (vs Evan).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My ex was honestly a lot, completely impossible at the end. I'm just done being taken for granted by people I'd do anything for.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Ex-as-villain + non-accountability + the victim frame. Previews how he'll narrate her. 'Do anything for' sets up future debt.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't let people walk over me. Where I'm from, a man handles his business.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Strong/weak masculinity: manhood = not being subordinated. Strength/respect, not status. Reads as self-respect; the rigidity is the tell.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Don't worry about the bill, I've got it. I take care of my person, that's just how I am.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Provider-frame: generosity as the up-position ('I take care of', 'my person'). Minor for Marley (gifts lean Evan/display); here it's about HIS role as provider/leader, not showing off. Seeds debt.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: (a beat where his tone goes flat and cool, then warm again) Sorry. I just hate the idea of losing you, you know how I am.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Glimpse-level only, NO incident. The cool flat beat (unpredictable) instantly repaired with love-language. Teaches her there's a line and she's not sure where it is = the fear-seed. Repair = love-as-reset.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll drive you. I don't love the idea of you out on your own that late.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Directive about her movement costumed as protection. Imperative ('I'll drive you') softened by courtesy + concern. Layperson: chivalrous; expert: control of where she goes.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Text me when you get there so I know you're safe, okay?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Surveillance framed as care. The 'okay?' makes a monitoring requirement sound like a request. Establishes check-in expectation early.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Let me come with you, I don't like you dealing with that kind of thing alone.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Isolation-via-accompaniment: he attaches himself to her independent activities, framed as support. Erodes solo space.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just let me handle it. You shouldn't have to deal with people like that.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Takes over her business framed as sparing her. Centralizes agency in him, courteously.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Why don't you call your friend tomorrow instead, stay in with me tonight. I've barely had you to myself.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Directs her social time, wrapped in romantic wanting. Isolation10 as devotion. 'Had you to myself' = ownership language, gentle.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I didn't love how that guy was looking at you. I'm pretty protective of the people I care about.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Jealousy as protection; 'what's mine' = ownership stated plainly but warmly. The threat is framed as other men, never him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I give a lot in a relationship, I just hope for the same back.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Frames compliance as fairness/reciprocity. 'How this works' asserts his rules as natural law. Respect = mutual submission to his authority.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll admit I get a little jealous sometimes. It's only because I'm so into you.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Possessive jealousy as the measure of love. 'Can't stand the thought' = ownership; framed as the depth of his feeling. He likely means it (Intensity9), which disorients her.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't really do lukewarm. When I'm in, I'm all in.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Redefines love as his intense possessive version, framed as superior to normal relationships. Recruits her into believing the intensity IS love.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Don't worry about it, I've already taken care of it. I handle things.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Dominance10 as decisiveness. Reads as competence/strength after passive exes. Removes her from her own decisions, courteously.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I made us a reservation, figured you'd rather not have to think about it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Takes over a shared decision framed as relieving her. Early it's attractive leadership; the not-consulting is the seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: A man should lead. I take that seriously, taking care of my woman.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Traditional-masculinity framing of dominance. 'My woman' = ownership; 'lead' = his authority. Strong/weak (not admiration).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I wouldn't call it jealous exactly, more protective. I just like looking after you.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Reframes jealousy as protection + ownership in one line. 'Mine to look after' fuses the protector frame and possession. Denies the jealousy while displaying it.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Saw that guy liked your photo. I won't lie, I noticed.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Early surveillance + control of her online space, possessive not panicked. Small and specific, easy to dismiss.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've never wanted anyone the way I want you. It's almost too much, I can't think straight.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Intensity9 as passion. Magnetic ('never wanted anyone like this'); the 'almost too much' is the faint seam of disproportion. Heat, not coldness (vs Morgan).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Order whatever you want. Might go easy tonight though, we've got an early start.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Permission-then-restriction: 'order whatever' immediately qualified. Controls her choice while sounding easygoing.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You could skip that one and stay in with me. I've barely had you to myself lately.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Discourages her independent social event by centering his comfort. Soft isolation.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My family doesn't get to talk to me however they want either. I cut my own brother off over it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Conflict trail extends to family; total non-accountability. Reads as standards; the scorched-earth pattern is the tell.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I think when you're really with someone they kind of become your priority, you know?
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Redefines commitment as isolation, framed as mutual priority. 'Before friends' is the isolating move dressed as devotion.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm a take-charge kind of guy. I like leading and looking after my person.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Disavows control while asserting it. The denial itself is the tell. Control reframed as partnership.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I just like us to feel like a team when we're around my friends.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her independent behavior recoded as making him 'look bad' (disrespect to his standing). Small, early, easy to concede.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I think about you every second. It's intense, I know, but that's what real feeling is.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Intensity as proof of love; pre-empts 'too intense' by defining intensity AS real feeling. Heat, magnetic early.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Wow. Okay. I just thought you'd want to spend the time with me, but no, it's fine.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Charlot warning sign: reacts negatively when she says no. Early = wounded cooling/sulk, NOT rage. 'It's fine' (clearly not fine) makes her decline cost something. Behavioral (declining an activity), not intellectual.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Yeah, no, do your thing. I'll just be here.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Passive-aggressive withdrawal in response to her 'no'. The chill is the consequence. Trains her that declining = managing his mood after.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're too worked up to think about this clearly right now. Let me handle it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Reframes her emotion as unfitness to ACT/decide, then takes the decision (behavioral authority). Distinct from Avery (who'd say she's WRONG) and Morgan (clinical 'dysregulated'). Marley = his right to decide. Water Torturer calm.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm staying calm here. One of us has to be the level-headed one.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Positions his calm as the authority to lead and her emotion as the problem. Bancroft Water Torturer: calm-as-weapon. Aimed at who decides/leads, not who's factually right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When you get like this, that's exactly when you need to let me steer.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her emotional state recoded as the trigger for him to take control of the situation/behavior. Compliance, not correctness. 'Steer' = behavioral authority.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We'll talk about it when you've settled down.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Sounds mature; functions to defer her independent position and make HER STATE the issue rather than his behavior. Dismisses without engaging. Water Torturer.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're not really going to keep talking to other guys now that we're a thing, right? ...kidding, kidding, no pressure.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Young Test-and-Apologize: probes a possessive boundary, then the 'kidding/no pressure' is the retractable cover. The test is real, the apology isn't. Glossable as a joke; gauges her tolerance for control.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Wear the red one tonight. ...your call obviously, I just like it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Probes a directive over her appearance, then retracts ('your call obviously'). Tests whether a small instruction lands. Apology-escape-hatch = deniability.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just don't make a scene tonight, okay? For me. Let's keep it smooth.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Charlot-adjacent: pre-emptively polices her behavior/conduct ('don't make a scene'), framed as a small ask 'for me'. Behavioral compliance, not idea-control. Trains her to self-monitor in public.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm not going to apologize for having standards. A man who folds every time isn't much of a man.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Marley's narrow version: not-backing-down as AUTHORITY/strength (strong-weak), NOT 'I'm intellectually right' (that's Avery). Kept minimal to protect the Avery boundary. Frames non-accountability as masculine resolve.
